articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Prioritizing Export and Green Steel
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM), focusing on scaling up production for export markets and integrating green hydrogen into hard-to-abate sectors like steel.
• Phase II aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential and strategic location.
• Key initiatives include dedicated production incentives for green hydrogen and electrolyzer manufacturing, alongside pilot projects for green steel and ammonia production.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, enhance energy security, and contribute significantly to climate change mitigation targets.
• This phase builds upon the initial success of NGHM, which focused on domestic production and capacity building.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched by India.
Phase II focuses on export and green steel.
Nodal Ministry for NGHM: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Key components include SIGHT program (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) and pilot projects.
India's target for green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Green Hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges for India in becoming a global hub for Green Hydrogen export.
Evaluate the role of policy incentives and technological advancements in integrating green hydrogen into industrial sectors like steel.
Examine the potential socio-economic impacts of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on job creation and regional development.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Landmark 'Right to Healthcare Act', Aiming for Universal Access
articles.explainer:
• After extensive deliberations, the Indian Parliament has passed the 'Right to Healthcare Act', enshrining universal access to quality healthcare as a fundamental right.
• The Act mandates government and private healthcare providers to offer emergency medical treatment without upfront payment and ensures a basic package of services for all citizens.
• It also establishes a robust grievance redressal mechanism and outlines provisions for increasing public health infrastructure and human resources.
• Critics raise concerns about funding mechanisms and the potential strain on existing healthcare systems, especially in states with limited resources.
• Proponents highlight its potential to reduce out-of-pocket expenditure, improve health outcomes, and address health inequities across the nation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
prelimsPoints: Right to Healthcare Act passed by Indian Parliament.
Aims for universal access to quality healthcare.
Mandates emergency treatment without upfront payment.
Establishes a grievance redressal mechanism.
Impact on public and private healthcare providers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of declaring healthcare a fundamental right in India, considering its constitutional and socio-economic dimensions.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to Healthcare Act', particularly regarding financial sustainability, infrastructure gaps, and human resource availability.
Evaluate the potential of this Act to address health disparities and improve public health outcomes across different regions and socio-economic strata in India.
Examine the role of the state and private sector in ensuring the successful implementation of universal healthcare provisions under the new Act.
articles.imageDescription: Doctors and patients in a hospital setting, symbolizing universal healthcare access.
articles.trivia: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, 'Protection of Life and Personal Liberty', has been interpreted by the Supreme Court to include the right to health.

articles.headline: India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit Concludes: Focus on Trade, Digital Cooperation, and Climate Action
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit concluded with significant agreements aimed at deepening cooperation across trade, digital technologies, and climate change mitigation.
• Leaders emphasized the importance of concluding the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations expeditiously, recognizing its potential to boost bilateral trade and investment.
• New initiatives were announced for collaboration in Artificial Intelligence, quantum computing, and cybersecurity, underscoring a shared vision for a secure and open digital future.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to the Paris Agreement, pledging enhanced cooperation on green technologies, sustainable finance, and renewable energy transition.
• The summit also addressed geopolitical developments, reiterating commitment to a rules-based international order and multilateralism.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit held.
Key areas of cooperation: trade, digital, climate.
Emphasis on concluding India-EU FTA.
New initiatives in AI, quantum computing, cybersecurity.
Reaffirmation of Paris Agreement commitments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-EU partnership in the evolving global geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges of the proposed India-EU Free Trade Agreement for both parties.
Examine how India and the EU can collaborate effectively on digital technologies and climate change to achieve shared development goals.
Evaluate the role of India-EU cooperation in strengthening multilateralism and addressing global challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2023-24.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties, Projects Steady Growth for Q3 FY26
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged, citing persistent global economic uncertainties and the need to support domestic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over volatile global commodity prices and geopolitical tensions, which could impact India's inflation trajectory.
• The central bank maintained its growth projection for Q3 FY26, attributing it to robust domestic demand, government capital expenditure, and strong performance in the services sector.
• Liquidity measures were also discussed, with the RBI reiterating its flexible approach to manage systemic liquidity conditions.
• Analysts suggest the RBI's stance indicates a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with growth revival in a challenging global environment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting outcome.
Repo rate maintained at current levels.
Reasons for maintaining repo rate: global uncertainties, growth support.
RBI's growth projection for Q3 FY26.
Factors influencing inflation trajectory (commodity prices, geopolitics).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current domestic and global economic conditions.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of fostering economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and ensuring financial stability in India.
Examine the interplay between global economic trends and India's domestic monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Coastal Security Bolstered with Induction of Advanced Patrol Vessels and Integrated Surveillance Systems
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) commissioned a new fleet of advanced offshore patrol vessels (OPVs) and launched an integrated coastal surveillance network (ICSN) as part of ongoing efforts to enhance maritime security.
• These measures aim to strengthen the surveillance capabilities along India's vast coastline, crucial for counter-terrorism, anti-smuggling operations, and search and rescue missions.
• The new OPVs are equipped with state-of-the-art navigation, communication, and weaponry systems, significantly boosting the ICG's operational reach and effectiveness.
• The ICSN integrates various sensors, radars, and real-time data sharing platforms, providing a comprehensive maritime domain awareness picture.
• This strategic upgrade underscores India's commitment to securing its maritime borders and protecting its exclusive economic zone (EEZ).
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Indian Coast Guard (ICG) inducts new offshore patrol vessels (OPVs).
Launch of integrated coastal surveillance network (ICSN).
Purpose: counter-terrorism, anti-smuggling, search & rescue.
OPVs equipped with advanced systems.
Role of ICG in securing India's maritime borders and EEZ.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges to India's coastal security and the importance of a robust maritime domain awareness strategy.
Analyze the significance of modernizing the Indian Coast Guard and integrating surveillance systems in safeguarding national interests.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current coastal security mechanisms in preventing infiltration, smuggling, and other maritime threats.
Suggest further measures and collaborations required to enhance India's comprehensive coastal security framework.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard offshore patrol vessel sailing in the open sea.
articles.trivia: The Indian Coast Guard was formally established on 1 February 1977 by the Coast Guard Act, 1978.

articles.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Stricter Conservation Norms for Western Ghats, Balancing Ecology and Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• A government-appointed expert panel submitted its report on the conservation of the Western Ghats, recommending stricter environmental protection norms for ecologically sensitive areas.
• The panel emphasized the need for a balanced approach, integrating ecological preservation with the sustainable livelihoods of local communities residing in the region.
• Key recommendations include designating certain areas as Ecologically Sensitive Zones (ESZs) with regulated development activities and promoting eco-tourism and sustainable agriculture.
• The report also highlighted the critical role of local governance and community participation in effective conservation strategies.
• Environmental groups welcomed the recommendations, while some local bodies and industries expressed concerns over potential restrictions on developmental projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Expert panel report on Western Ghats conservation submitted.
Recommendations for stricter environmental protection norms.
Concept of Ecologically Sensitive Zones (ESZs).
Emphasis on balancing ecology and local livelihoods.
Previous committees: Gadgil Committee and Kasturirangan Committee.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the challenges in implementing conservation policies in ecologically sensitive regions, particularly concerning the conflict between development and environment.
Evaluate the recommendations of the expert panel, drawing parallels and contrasts with previous committees (Gadgil, Kasturirangan).
Examine the role of local communities and governance in achieving sustainable development and conservation goals in the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with misty mountains.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is one of the eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator program.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a key step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle.
• This technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by enabling multiple reuses of rocket stages, similar to commercial space ventures globally.
• The RLV program is central to ISRO's long-term strategy for sustained and cost-effective space exploration and commercial satellite launches.
• Scientists lauded the precision engineering and complex flight dynamics involved in achieving the desired landing parameters.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator test.
Focus on controlled landing experiment.
Goal: reduce cost of space access.
Significance for future space missions and commercial launches.
RLV-TD is a winged body aerospace vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its potential impact on global space access.
Analyze the technological challenges and strategic advantages associated with developing indigenous RLV capabilities.
Evaluate how RLV technology can contribute to India's economic growth through commercial space ventures and international collaborations.
Examine the future trajectory of ISRO's space exploration efforts in light of advancements in reusable launch systems.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) returning to Earth.
articles.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested the RLV-TD (Hypersonic Flight Experiment) in 2016.

articles.headline: Veteran Nagaland MLA Imkong L. Imchen Passes Away at 75
articles.explainer:
• Imkong L. Imchen, a prominent political figure and long-serving Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Nagaland, passed away at the age of 75.
• Imchen served multiple terms as an MLA since 2003, representing the Jangpetkong constituency, and held various ministerial portfolios during his distinguished career.
• He was known for his advocacy for Naga peace process, regional development, and the welfare of his constituents.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Nagaland politics, with condolences pouring in from across the political spectrum.
• Imchen's contributions to state governance and efforts towards stability in the sensitive region are widely acknowledged.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: Imkong L. Imchen: Nagaland MLA.
Served since 2003.
Represented Jangpetkong constituency.
Known for involvement in Naga peace process.
articles.trivia: Nagaland became the 16th state of the Indian Union on 1 December 1963.

articles.headline: Environmental Stalwart Saalumarada Thimmakka Dies at 114, Leaves Legacy of Greenery
articles.explainer:
• Saalumarada Thimmakka, the revered environmentalist from Karnataka known for planting and nurturing thousands of trees, passed away at the remarkable age of 114.
• Affectionately known as 'Saalumarada' (rows of trees), she gained national and international recognition for her selfless efforts, particularly planting over 8,000 trees across 80 years, mainly banyan trees along a 4 km stretch of highway.
• Her work earned her the Padma Shri, India's fourth-highest civilian award, and numerous other accolades, inspiring countless individuals to take up environmental conservation.
• Thimmakka's life story is a testament to grassroots environmentalism and the profound impact a single individual can have on ecological restoration.
• Her legacy continues to motivate community-led tree plantation drives and environmental awareness campaigns.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Saalumarada Thimmakka: renowned environmentalist.
Known for planting thousands of trees, especially banyan.
Recipient of Padma Shri award.
Associated with Karnataka.
Symbol of grassroots environmentalism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of individual and community-led initiatives like Saalumarada Thimmakka's in addressing environmental degradation and promoting conservation.
Analyze how grassroots movements contribute to broader environmental policy and public awareness in India.
Evaluate the role of traditional knowledge and local communities in sustainable forest management and biodiversity protection, drawing insights from Thimmakka's life.
Examine the challenges faced by environmental activists and the importance of recognizing their contributions through awards like Padma Shri.
articles.imageDescription: Saalumarada Thimmakka standing amidst lush green trees she planted.
articles.trivia: Despite being illiterate, Thimmakka's dedication to planting trees earned her global recognition, including being named in BBC's list of 100 most influential women.

articles.headline: Golden Age Icon Kamini Kaushal Passes Away at 98, Leaving a Rich Cinematic Legacy
articles.explainer:
• Veteran actress Kamini Kaushal, a towering figure of Hindi cinema's golden era, passed away peacefully at the age of 98.
• Known for her versatile performances in classics like 'Neecha Nagar' (1946), 'Biraj Bahu' (1954), and later acclaimed for her roles in films like 'Kabir Singh' (2019), she had a career spanning over seven decades.
• Kaushal's debut film 'Neecha Nagar' won the Palme d'Or at the first Cannes Film Festival in 1946, marking a historic moment for Indian cinema.
• Her passing marks the end of an illustrious chapter in Indian film history, celebrated for her grace, talent, and contribution to the performing arts.
• She remained active in cinema well into her nineties, bridging generations of actors and filmmakers.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: Kamini Kaushal: veteran Indian actress.
Associated with Hindi cinema's golden era.
Debut film 'Neecha Nagar' won Palme d'Or at Cannes (1946).
Notable films: 'Biraj Bahu', 'Kabir Singh'.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of young Kamini Kaushal.
articles.trivia: Kamini Kaushal was the first Indian actress to win the Best Actress award at the Filmfare Awards for her role in 'Biraj Bahu' (1955).

articles.headline: India Hosts Global Summit on Digital Public Infrastructure, Advocates for Open-Source and Inclusive Models
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting a high-level Global Summit on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), bringing together policymakers, tech experts, and international organizations to discuss future-proof digital ecosystems.
• The summit aims to share India's success story with its stack of DPI (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) and advocate for open-source, interoperable, and inclusive digital models globally.
• Discussions focused on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, accessible healthcare, education, and transparent governance in developing nations.
• Several countries expressed interest in adopting elements of the 'India Stack' to build their own digital public goods, facilitating cross-border digital payments and identity verification.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing ethical AI, data privacy, and global cooperation in building resilient digital infrastructures.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
IT, Computers, Robotics, Nanotechnology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Global Summit on Digital Public Infrastructure hosted by India.
India's role in advocating for open-source DPI models.
Examples of India's DPI: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker.
Focus areas: financial inclusion, healthcare, education, governance.
Joint declaration on ethical AI and data privacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for governance and socio-economic development.
Analyze the key components of the 'India Stack' and how it serves as a model for other developing nations in building inclusive digital ecosystems.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for global cooperation in establishing interoperable and secure DPI, considering aspects like data privacy and ethical AI.
Examine how India's leadership in DPI can enhance its soft power and technological diplomacy on the international stage.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of interconnected digital networks and services.
articles.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) recorded over 11.76 billion transactions in October 2024, solidifying its position as a leading digital payment system globally.

articles.headline: Government Initiates Comprehensive Review of Forest Rights Act Implementation to Address Tribal Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs announced a comprehensive review of the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, aiming to address persistent challenges faced by tribal communities.
• The review will assess the extent of individual and community forest rights recognized, identify bottlenecks in the claims process, and ensure effective participation of Gram Sabhas.
• Concerns have been raised by tribal rights activists regarding slow progress in granting titles, rejections of legitimate claims, and lack of awareness among beneficiaries.
• The government plans to streamline the process, enhance capacity building for local authorities, and leverage technology for better record-keeping and grievance redressal.
• This initiative underscores the commitment to empower tribal populations and protect their traditional rights over forest lands and resources.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tribal Affairs reviews Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
Aims to address challenges in FRA implementation.
Focus on Individual and Community Forest Rights (IFR & CFR).
Role of Gram Sabhas in FRA.
Concerns: slow progress, claim rejections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and significance of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, in empowering tribal communities and forest dwellers.
Analyze the major challenges and bottlenecks in the effective implementation of the FRA across different states in India.
Evaluate the impact of the FRA on tribal livelihoods, forest conservation, and the recognition of traditional land rights.
Suggest policy measures and administrative reforms to strengthen the implementation of the FRA and ensure social justice for tribal populations.
articles.imageDescription: Tribal women collecting forest produce in a traditional setting.
articles.trivia: The Forest Rights Act recognizes the rights of forest-dwelling Scheduled Tribes and other traditional forest dwellers to forest land and resources, which were denied to them for centuries.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Meeting Concludes Amidst Calls for Enhanced Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission between India and Pakistan concluded, with both sides discussing various technical aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The agenda included sharing hydrological data, inspections of projects on both sides, and addressing concerns related to the design of certain hydropower projects.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, officials highlighted the constructive dialogue and the commitment to uphold the provisions of the IWT, a crucial water-sharing agreement.
• The meeting comes amidst ongoing efforts to improve bilateral relations, with water cooperation being a vital component of regional stability.
• Experts emphasize the IWT's resilience as a successful model for transboundary water management despite geopolitical tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Annual meeting of Permanent Indus Commission.
Between India and Pakistan.
Discusses Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
Focus on hydrological data and project inspections.
IWT allocates Eastern Rivers to India, Western Rivers to Pakistan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and successes of the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving disputes and ensuring the smooth functioning of the IWT.
Evaluate the role of water diplomacy in maintaining regional stability and fostering cooperation between riparian states.
Examine the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the long-term viability and potential renegotiation of transboundary water treaties like the IWT.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River System and the distribution of rivers under the Indus Waters Treaty.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960 and brokered by the World Bank. It is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world.

articles.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules, Emphasizing Producer Responsibility and Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has proposed significant amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, aiming for a more robust and circular economy approach.
• The proposed changes emphasize extended producer responsibility (EPR), stricter collection targets, and incentives for formal recycling channels.
• New provisions include mandatory digital manifests for e-waste movement and greater accountability for bulk consumers to ensure proper disposal and recycling.
• The amendments seek to address the growing challenge of e-waste generation, promote resource efficiency, and mitigate environmental pollution from improper handling.
• Stakeholders, including manufacturers, recyclers, and environmental NGOs, are invited to provide feedback on the draft rules.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Amendments proposed for E-Waste Management Rules.
Focus on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
Aims for circular economy and formal recycling.
New provisions for digital manifests and bulk consumer accountability.
Ministry involved: MoEFCC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of e-waste management in India and its environmental and health implications.
Analyze the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in the context of e-waste and its effectiveness in promoting circular economy principles.
Evaluate the proposed amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, highlighting their potential to streamline collection, recycling, and disposal processes.
Examine the role of technology, consumer awareness, and informal sector integration in building a sustainable e-waste management ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets symbolizing e-waste.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world.

articles.headline: Ministry of AYUSH Launches 'Ayurveda for Public Health' Initiative to Integrate Traditional Medicine
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of AYUSH inaugurated the 'Ayurveda for Public Health' initiative, aimed at integrating traditional Indian medicine systems into mainstream healthcare delivery.
• The program focuses on preventive healthcare, promotion of healthy lifestyles, and management of chronic diseases through Ayurvedic principles and practices.
• Key components include establishing Ayurveda units in primary health centers, training healthcare professionals, and conducting awareness campaigns on Ayurvedic remedies and diets.
• This initiative aligns with the National Health Policy's goal of pluralistic healthcare and leveraging the strengths of AYUSH systems.
• Pilot projects are being launched in several districts to assess the impact and scalability of the integrated approach.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Ministry of AYUSH launches 'Ayurveda for Public Health'.
Aims to integrate Ayurveda into mainstream healthcare.
Focus on preventive healthcare and chronic disease management.
Pilot projects in districts.
Aligned with National Health Policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of integrating traditional Indian medicine systems like Ayurveda into mainstream public health programs.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in promoting preventive healthcare and healthy lifestyles through AYUSH practices.
Evaluate the significance of the 'Ayurveda for Public Health' initiative in achieving universal health coverage and addressing the burden of non-communicable diseases.
Examine the scientific validation and standardization requirements for effectively integrating traditional medicine into modern healthcare frameworks.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting Ayurvedic herbs and traditional healing symbols.
articles.trivia: AYUSH stands for Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy – the six Indian systems of medicine.

articles.headline: India and Maldives Reaffirm Commitment to Strengthen Bilateral Ties and Enhance Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Maldives held discussions, reaffirming their commitment to further strengthen bilateral ties, with a particular focus on security cooperation.
• The talks covered various aspects, including defense training, maritime security, counter-terrorism efforts, and intelligence sharing.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of maintaining peace and stability in the Indian Ocean Region, recognizing their shared strategic interests.
• Discussions also touched upon infrastructure development projects in Maldives supported by India and enhancing people-to-people connections through cultural exchanges and tourism.
• The meeting concluded with a joint statement highlighting mutual respect, understanding, and a shared vision for a prosperous and secure Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Maldives high-level discussions.
Focus on strengthening bilateral ties and security cooperation.
Areas: defense training, maritime security, counter-terrorism.
Importance of peace in Indian Ocean Region.
India's support for infrastructure in Maldives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Maldives for India's security and economic interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the various facets of India-Maldives bilateral cooperation, including defense, development, and cultural exchanges.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in maintaining a strong and stable relationship between India and Maldives amidst regional geopolitical dynamics.
Examine how India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy is reflected in its engagement with island nations like Maldives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Maldives waving against a scenic backdrop.
articles.trivia: India was among the first countries to recognize Maldives' independence in 1965.

articles.headline: Researchers Discover New Species of Amphibian in Eastern Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Significance
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers has announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the unexplored regions of the Eastern Ghats, adding to India's rich biodiversity.
• The newly identified species, a type of frog, exhibits unique morphological and genetic characteristics, distinguishing it from known species in the region.
• This discovery underscores the ecological importance of the Eastern Ghats, often overshadowed by the Western Ghats, as a significant biodiversity hotspot requiring focused conservation efforts.
• Scientists emphasize the need for extensive surveys and habitat protection to document and preserve the region's diverse flora and fauna before they are lost to habitat degradation.
• The finding contributes valuable data to the understanding of amphibian evolution and distribution in peninsular India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New amphibian species discovered in Eastern Ghats.
Highlights Eastern Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot.
Significance for amphibian evolution and distribution.
Need for conservation and habitat protection.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up image of a newly discovered frog species.
articles.trivia: Amphibians are considered excellent bio-indicators, meaning their presence or absence can indicate the health of an ecosystem.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Boosts Innovation, Supports Over 1000 Startups in Key Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) has successfully supported over 1000 startups across various sectors, demonstrating its crucial role in fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in India.
• Launched by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), the scheme provides financial assistance to eligible startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• The scheme has prioritized startups in deep technology, social impact, waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, agriculture, and healthcare.
• Officials noted the scheme's instrumental role in bridging the early-stage funding gap, particularly for startups in tier-2 and tier-3 cities.
• This initiative is a cornerstone of the 'Startup India' movement, aiming to create a robust ecosystem for nascent businesses.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
keyTerms: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) achievements.
Supports startups for proof of concept, prototype, market-entry.
Implemented by DPIIT.
Focus sectors: deep tech, social impact, waste management, etc.
Aims to bridge early-stage funding gap.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of government-backed seed funding initiatives like SISFS in promoting entrepreneurship and innovation in India.
Analyze how the Startup India movement is contributing to job creation, economic diversification, and technological advancement.
Evaluate the challenges faced by early-stage startups in India and how schemes like SISFS address these issues, particularly in non-metro cities.
Examine the role of deep technology startups in solving complex societal problems and driving India's future economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse individuals collaborating in a modern office space, symbolizing startup culture.
articles.trivia: The 'Startup India' initiative was launched on January 16, 2016, to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in the country.

articles.headline: National Climate-Resilient Agriculture Mission Launched to Combat Climate Change Impacts on Farming
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare officially launched the National Climate-Resilient Agriculture Mission (NCRAM) to equip Indian farmers against the adverse effects of climate change.
• The mission focuses on developing and deploying climate-smart agricultural practices, drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and agro-advisory services.
• It aims to enhance the adaptive capacity of small and marginal farmers, improve soil health, and reduce greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural activities.
• NCRAM will integrate traditional knowledge with modern scientific approaches, promoting sustainable farming systems across vulnerable regions.
• Pilot projects are initiated in climate-vulnerable districts to demonstrate effective strategies and build local capacities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

Agriculture: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
prelimsPoints: National Climate-Resilient Agriculture Mission (NCRAM) launched.
Ministry: Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
Focus: climate-smart agriculture, drought-resistant crops, water management.
Aims to enhance adaptive capacity of farmers.
Integrates traditional and modern approaches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted impacts of climate change on Indian agriculture and the imperative for climate-resilient farming practices.
Analyze the key components and expected outcomes of the National Climate-Resilient Agriculture Mission (NCRAM) in ensuring food security and farmers' livelihoods.
Evaluate the role of technology, research, and traditional knowledge in developing and disseminating climate-smart agricultural solutions.
Examine the challenges in implementing climate-resilient agriculture across diverse agro-climatic zones in India and suggest strategies for effective adoption.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with modern irrigation techniques under a bright sky.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts, with agriculture supporting over half its population.

highlights: India launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting export markets and green steel integration.
Parliament passes landmark 'Right to Healthcare Act' aiming for universal access and comprehensive health services.
India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit concludes with focus on trade, digital cooperation, and climate action, pushing for FTA.
ISRO successfully conducts a critical test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator, advancing cost-effective space access.
Veteran environmentalist Saalumarada Thimmakka passes away at 114, leaving behind a legacy of planting thousands of trees.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) Phase II, consider the following statements:
1. It primarily focuses on reducing domestic consumption of green hydrogen.
2. It aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen export.
3. Integration of green hydrogen into hard-to-abate sectors like steel is a key objective.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Phase II focuses on scaling up production for export markets, implying increased production, not reduced consumption. Statement 2 is correct, as the mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 3 is correct, as integrating green hydrogen into hard-to-abate sectors like steel is a primary focus.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Right to Healthcare Act', recently passed by the Indian Parliament, primarily aims to:
(a) Nationalize all private healthcare facilities across the country.
(b) Ensure universal access to quality healthcare as a fundamental right.
(c) Mandate free healthcare for all citizens through private insurance.
(d) Establish a single-payer healthcare system managed by the central government.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Ensure universal access to quality healthcare as a fundamental right.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Right to Healthcare Act' aims to enshrine universal access to quality healthcare as a fundamental right, mandating services like emergency treatment without upfront payment and establishing grievance redressal mechanisms. It does not aim to nationalize private facilities, rely solely on private insurance for free care, or establish a single-payer system.

prelimsMocks.question: Saalumarada Thimmakka, who recently passed away, was renowned for her contributions in which field?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Saalumarada Thimmakka was a legendary environmentalist from Karnataka, famous for planting and nurturing thousands of trees, earning her the Padma Shri award and international recognition for her selfless efforts in environmental conservation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT):
1. It was signed between India and China in 1960.
2. The treaty allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to Pakistan.
3. The Permanent Indus Commission facilitates discussions and resolves technical aspects of the treaty.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the IWT was signed between India and Pakistan. Statement 2 is incorrect; the treaty allocates the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan. Statement 3 is correct, as the Permanent Indus Commission is the body responsible for implementing and resolving technical issues related to the treaty.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of India's 'India Stack' Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar (digital identity), UPI (digital payments), and DigiLocker (digital document wallet) are core components of the India Stack, which provides a layered digital public infrastructure. The National Optical Fibre Network (NOFN), now BharatNet, is a physical infrastructure project for broadband connectivity, not a direct component of the 'India Stack' in the same functional sense as the others.

mainsMocks.question: India's pursuit of green hydrogen as a key energy source is a strategic imperative for both energy security and climate action. Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in green hydrogen production and export, particularly considering its role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its strategic importance. Discuss opportunities like abundant renewable energy, cost reduction potential, and decarbonization of industries (green steel, ammonia). Highlight challenges such as high initial capital cost, technology scaling, infrastructure development, water availability, and international competition. Conclude with policy measures and international collaborations needed.

mainsMocks.question: The newly passed 'Right to Healthcare Act' marks a significant step towards universal health coverage in India. Critically analyze the potential impacts of this legislation on the Indian healthcare system, focusing on its implications for accessibility, affordability, and quality of care. What challenges might arise in its implementation, and how can they be addressed? (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by defining the 'Right to Healthcare' and its constitutional context. Discuss positive impacts on access (emergency care, basic package), affordability (reduced out-of-pocket expenses), and quality (standardization, grievance redressal). Critically examine challenges: funding, infrastructure gaps, human resource shortages, integration of private sector, regulatory complexities, and center-state coordination. Suggest solutions like innovative financing, public-private partnerships, capacity building, and robust monitoring.

mainsMocks.question: The Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, was enacted to correct historical injustices against forest-dwelling communities. Despite its noble objectives, its implementation has faced several hurdles. Examine the key provisions of the FRA and critically evaluate the challenges in its effective implementation. Suggest measures to strengthen the recognition and enforcement of tribal rights under this Act. (10 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce FRA's purpose and key provisions (Individual Forest Rights, Community Forest Rights, Gram Sabha's role). Discuss implementation challenges: slow processing of claims, high rejection rates, lack of awareness, bureaucratic hurdles, conflict with forest department, and commercial interests. Suggest measures such as streamlining the claims process, capacity building for local authorities, use of technology, strengthening Gram Sabha's role, and convergence with other development schemes.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission's Phase II aims to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen export.
The 'Right to Healthcare Act' seeks to make universal access to quality healthcare a fundamental right in India.
Saalumarada Thimmakka, an environmentalist, was known for planting thousands of trees, especially banyan trees.
ISRO successfully tested its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator for cost-effective space access.
The Permanent Indus Commission meeting discusses technical aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty between India and Pakistan.
The Ministry of AYUSH launched 'Ayurveda for Public Health' to integrate traditional medicine into mainstream healthcare.
The Eastern Ghats, a biodiversity hotspot, saw the discovery of a new amphibian species, highlighting its ecological significance.
The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) supports early-stage startups, bridging critical funding gaps.
The National Climate-Resilient Agriculture Mission (NCRAM) aims to protect Indian farming from climate change impacts.
Kamini Kaushal, a veteran actress, debuted in 'Neecha Nagar' which won the Palme d'Or at the first Cannes Film Festival.

GS1.headline: Golden Age Icon Kamini Kaushal Passes Away at 98, Leaving a Rich Cinematic Legacy
GS1.explainer:
• Veteran actress Kamini Kaushal, a towering figure of Hindi cinema's golden era, passed away peacefully at the age of 98.
• Known for her versatile performances in classics like 'Neecha Nagar' (1946), 'Biraj Bahu' (1954), and later acclaimed for her roles in films like 'Kabir Singh' (2019), she had a career spanning over seven decades.
• Kaushal's debut film 'Neecha Nagar' won the Palme d'Or at the first Cannes Film Festival in 1946, marking a historic moment for Indian cinema.
• Her passing marks the end of an illustrious chapter in Indian film history, celebrated for her grace, talent, and contribution to the performing arts.
• She remained active in cinema well into her nineties, bridging generations of actors and filmmakers.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: Kamini Kaushal: veteran Indian actress.
Associated with Hindi cinema's golden era.
Debut film 'Neecha Nagar' won Palme d'Or at Cannes (1946).
Notable films: 'Biraj Bahu', 'Kabir Singh'.
GS1.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of young Kamini Kaushal.
GS1.trivia: Kamini Kaushal was the first Indian actress to win the Best Actress award at the Filmfare Awards for her role in 'Biraj Bahu' (1955).

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Landmark 'Right to Healthcare Act', Aiming for Universal Access
GS2.explainer:
• After extensive deliberations, the Indian Parliament has passed the 'Right to Healthcare Act', enshrining universal access to quality healthcare as a fundamental right.
• The Act mandates government and private healthcare providers to offer emergency medical treatment without upfront payment and ensures a basic package of services for all citizens.
• It also establishes a robust grievance redressal mechanism and outlines provisions for increasing public health infrastructure and human resources.
• Critics raise concerns about funding mechanisms and the potential strain on existing healthcare systems, especially in states with limited resources.
• Proponents highlight its potential to reduce out-of-pocket expenditure, improve health outcomes, and address health inequities across the nation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
prelimsPoints: Right to Healthcare Act passed by Indian Parliament.
Aims for universal access to quality healthcare.
Mandates emergency treatment without upfront payment.
Establishes a grievance redressal mechanism.
Impact on public and private healthcare providers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of declaring healthcare a fundamental right in India, considering its constitutional and socio-economic dimensions.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to Healthcare Act', particularly regarding financial sustainability, infrastructure gaps, and human resource availability.
Evaluate the potential of this Act to address health disparities and improve public health outcomes across different regions and socio-economic strata in India.
Examine the role of the state and private sector in ensuring the successful implementation of universal healthcare provisions under the new Act.
GS2.imageDescription: Doctors and patients in a hospital setting, symbolizing universal healthcare access.
GS2.trivia: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, 'Protection of Life and Personal Liberty', has been interpreted by the Supreme Court to include the right to health.

GS2.headline: India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit Concludes: Focus on Trade, Digital Cooperation, and Climate Action
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit concluded with significant agreements aimed at deepening cooperation across trade, digital technologies, and climate change mitigation.
• Leaders emphasized the importance of concluding the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations expeditiously, recognizing its potential to boost bilateral trade and investment.
• New initiatives were announced for collaboration in Artificial Intelligence, quantum computing, and cybersecurity, underscoring a shared vision for a secure and open digital future.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to the Paris Agreement, pledging enhanced cooperation on green technologies, sustainable finance, and renewable energy transition.
• The summit also addressed geopolitical developments, reiterating commitment to a rules-based international order and multilateralism.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-EU Strategic Partnership Summit held.
Key areas of cooperation: trade, digital, climate.
Emphasis on concluding India-EU FTA.
New initiatives in AI, quantum computing, cybersecurity.
Reaffirmation of Paris Agreement commitments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-EU partnership in the evolving global geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges of the proposed India-EU Free Trade Agreement for both parties.
Examine how India and the EU can collaborate effectively on digital technologies and climate change to achieve shared development goals.
Evaluate the role of India-EU cooperation in strengthening multilateralism and addressing global challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2023-24.

GS2.headline: Veteran Nagaland MLA Imkong L. Imchen Passes Away at 75
GS2.explainer:
• Imkong L. Imchen, a prominent political figure and long-serving Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Nagaland, passed away at the age of 75.
• Imchen served multiple terms as an MLA since 2003, representing the Jangpetkong constituency, and held various ministerial portfolios during his distinguished career.
• He was known for his advocacy for Naga peace process, regional development, and the welfare of his constituents.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Nagaland politics, with condolences pouring in from across the political spectrum.
• Imchen's contributions to state governance and efforts towards stability in the sensitive region are widely acknowledged.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: Imkong L. Imchen: Nagaland MLA.
Served since 2003.
Represented Jangpetkong constituency.
Known for involvement in Naga peace process.
GS2.trivia: Nagaland became the 16th state of the Indian Union on 1 December 1963.

GS2.headline: Government Initiates Comprehensive Review of Forest Rights Act Implementation to Address Tribal Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs announced a comprehensive review of the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, aiming to address persistent challenges faced by tribal communities.
• The review will assess the extent of individual and community forest rights recognized, identify bottlenecks in the claims process, and ensure effective participation of Gram Sabhas.
• Concerns have been raised by tribal rights activists regarding slow progress in granting titles, rejections of legitimate claims, and lack of awareness among beneficiaries.
• The government plans to streamline the process, enhance capacity building for local authorities, and leverage technology for better record-keeping and grievance redressal.
• This initiative underscores the commitment to empower tribal populations and protect their traditional rights over forest lands and resources.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tribal Affairs reviews Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
Aims to address challenges in FRA implementation.
Focus on Individual and Community Forest Rights (IFR & CFR).
Role of Gram Sabhas in FRA.
Concerns: slow progress, claim rejections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and significance of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, in empowering tribal communities and forest dwellers.
Analyze the major challenges and bottlenecks in the effective implementation of the FRA across different states in India.
Evaluate the impact of the FRA on tribal livelihoods, forest conservation, and the recognition of traditional land rights.
Suggest policy measures and administrative reforms to strengthen the implementation of the FRA and ensure social justice for tribal populations.
GS2.imageDescription: Tribal women collecting forest produce in a traditional setting.
GS2.trivia: The Forest Rights Act recognizes the rights of forest-dwelling Scheduled Tribes and other traditional forest dwellers to forest land and resources, which were denied to them for centuries.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Meeting Concludes Amidst Calls for Enhanced Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission between India and Pakistan concluded, with both sides discussing various technical aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The agenda included sharing hydrological data, inspections of projects on both sides, and addressing concerns related to the design of certain hydropower projects.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, officials highlighted the constructive dialogue and the commitment to uphold the provisions of the IWT, a crucial water-sharing agreement.
• The meeting comes amidst ongoing efforts to improve bilateral relations, with water cooperation being a vital component of regional stability.
• Experts emphasize the IWT's resilience as a successful model for transboundary water management despite geopolitical tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Annual meeting of Permanent Indus Commission.
Between India and Pakistan.
Discusses Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
Focus on hydrological data and project inspections.
IWT allocates Eastern Rivers to India, Western Rivers to Pakistan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and successes of the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving disputes and ensuring the smooth functioning of the IWT.
Evaluate the role of water diplomacy in maintaining regional stability and fostering cooperation between riparian states.
Examine the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the long-term viability and potential renegotiation of transboundary water treaties like the IWT.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River System and the distribution of rivers under the Indus Waters Treaty.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960 and brokered by the World Bank. It is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world.

GS2.headline: Ministry of AYUSH Launches 'Ayurveda for Public Health' Initiative to Integrate Traditional Medicine
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of AYUSH inaugurated the 'Ayurveda for Public Health' initiative, aimed at integrating traditional Indian medicine systems into mainstream healthcare delivery.
• The program focuses on preventive healthcare, promotion of healthy lifestyles, and management of chronic diseases through Ayurvedic principles and practices.
• Key components include establishing Ayurveda units in primary health centers, training healthcare professionals, and conducting awareness campaigns on Ayurvedic remedies and diets.
• This initiative aligns with the National Health Policy's goal of pluralistic healthcare and leveraging the strengths of AYUSH systems.
• Pilot projects are being launched in several districts to assess the impact and scalability of the integrated approach.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Ministry of AYUSH launches 'Ayurveda for Public Health'.
Aims to integrate Ayurveda into mainstream healthcare.
Focus on preventive healthcare and chronic disease management.
Pilot projects in districts.
Aligned with National Health Policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of integrating traditional Indian medicine systems like Ayurveda into mainstream public health programs.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in promoting preventive healthcare and healthy lifestyles through AYUSH practices.
Evaluate the significance of the 'Ayurveda for Public Health' initiative in achieving universal health coverage and addressing the burden of non-communicable diseases.
Examine the scientific validation and standardization requirements for effectively integrating traditional medicine into modern healthcare frameworks.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting Ayurvedic herbs and traditional healing symbols.
GS2.trivia: AYUSH stands for Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy – the six Indian systems of medicine.

GS2.headline: India and Maldives Reaffirm Commitment to Strengthen Bilateral Ties and Enhance Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Maldives held discussions, reaffirming their commitment to further strengthen bilateral ties, with a particular focus on security cooperation.
• The talks covered various aspects, including defense training, maritime security, counter-terrorism efforts, and intelligence sharing.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of maintaining peace and stability in the Indian Ocean Region, recognizing their shared strategic interests.
• Discussions also touched upon infrastructure development projects in Maldives supported by India and enhancing people-to-people connections through cultural exchanges and tourism.
• The meeting concluded with a joint statement highlighting mutual respect, understanding, and a shared vision for a prosperous and secure Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Maldives high-level discussions.
Focus on strengthening bilateral ties and security cooperation.
Areas: defense training, maritime security, counter-terrorism.
Importance of peace in Indian Ocean Region.
India's support for infrastructure in Maldives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Maldives for India's security and economic interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the various facets of India-Maldives bilateral cooperation, including defense, development, and cultural exchanges.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in maintaining a strong and stable relationship between India and Maldives amidst regional geopolitical dynamics.
Examine how India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy is reflected in its engagement with island nations like Maldives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Maldives waving against a scenic backdrop.
GS2.trivia: India was among the first countries to recognize Maldives' independence in 1965.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Prioritizing Export and Green Steel
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM), focusing on scaling up production for export markets and integrating green hydrogen into hard-to-abate sectors like steel.
• Phase II aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential and strategic location.
• Key initiatives include dedicated production incentives for green hydrogen and electrolyzer manufacturing, alongside pilot projects for green steel and ammonia production.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, enhance energy security, and contribute significantly to climate change mitigation targets.
• This phase builds upon the initial success of NGHM, which focused on domestic production and capacity building.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched by India.
Phase II focuses on export and green steel.
Nodal Ministry for NGHM: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Key components include SIGHT program (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) and pilot projects.
India's target for green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Green Hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges for India in becoming a global hub for Green Hydrogen export.
Evaluate the role of policy incentives and technological advancements in integrating green hydrogen into industrial sectors like steel.
Examine the potential socio-economic impacts of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on job creation and regional development.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties, Projects Steady Growth for Q3 FY26
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged, citing persistent global economic uncertainties and the need to support domestic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over volatile global commodity prices and geopolitical tensions, which could impact India's inflation trajectory.
• The central bank maintained its growth projection for Q3 FY26, attributing it to robust domestic demand, government capital expenditure, and strong performance in the services sector.
• Liquidity measures were also discussed, with the RBI reiterating its flexible approach to manage systemic liquidity conditions.
• Analysts suggest the RBI's stance indicates a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with growth revival in a challenging global environment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting outcome.
Repo rate maintained at current levels.
Reasons for maintaining repo rate: global uncertainties, growth support.
RBI's growth projection for Q3 FY26.
Factors influencing inflation trajectory (commodity prices, geopolitics).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current domestic and global economic conditions.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of fostering economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and ensuring financial stability in India.
Examine the interplay between global economic trends and India's domestic monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Coastal Security Bolstered with Induction of Advanced Patrol Vessels and Integrated Surveillance Systems
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) commissioned a new fleet of advanced offshore patrol vessels (OPVs) and launched an integrated coastal surveillance network (ICSN) as part of ongoing efforts to enhance maritime security.
• These measures aim to strengthen the surveillance capabilities along India's vast coastline, crucial for counter-terrorism, anti-smuggling operations, and search and rescue missions.
• The new OPVs are equipped with state-of-the-art navigation, communication, and weaponry systems, significantly boosting the ICG's operational reach and effectiveness.
• The ICSN integrates various sensors, radars, and real-time data sharing platforms, providing a comprehensive maritime domain awareness picture.
• This strategic upgrade underscores India's commitment to securing its maritime borders and protecting its exclusive economic zone (EEZ).
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Indian Coast Guard (ICG) inducts new offshore patrol vessels (OPVs).
Launch of integrated coastal surveillance network (ICSN).
Purpose: counter-terrorism, anti-smuggling, search & rescue.
OPVs equipped with advanced systems.
Role of ICG in securing India's maritime borders and EEZ.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges to India's coastal security and the importance of a robust maritime domain awareness strategy.
Analyze the significance of modernizing the Indian Coast Guard and integrating surveillance systems in safeguarding national interests.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current coastal security mechanisms in preventing infiltration, smuggling, and other maritime threats.
Suggest further measures and collaborations required to enhance India's comprehensive coastal security framework.
GS3.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard offshore patrol vessel sailing in the open sea.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Coast Guard was formally established on 1 February 1977 by the Coast Guard Act, 1978.

GS3.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Stricter Conservation Norms for Western Ghats, Balancing Ecology and Livelihoods
GS3.explainer:
• A government-appointed expert panel submitted its report on the conservation of the Western Ghats, recommending stricter environmental protection norms for ecologically sensitive areas.
• The panel emphasized the need for a balanced approach, integrating ecological preservation with the sustainable livelihoods of local communities residing in the region.
• Key recommendations include designating certain areas as Ecologically Sensitive Zones (ESZs) with regulated development activities and promoting eco-tourism and sustainable agriculture.
• The report also highlighted the critical role of local governance and community participation in effective conservation strategies.
• Environmental groups welcomed the recommendations, while some local bodies and industries expressed concerns over potential restrictions on developmental projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Expert panel report on Western Ghats conservation submitted.
Recommendations for stricter environmental protection norms.
Concept of Ecologically Sensitive Zones (ESZs).
Emphasis on balancing ecology and local livelihoods.
Previous committees: Gadgil Committee and Kasturirangan Committee.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the challenges in implementing conservation policies in ecologically sensitive regions, particularly concerning the conflict between development and environment.
Evaluate the recommendations of the expert panel, drawing parallels and contrasts with previous committees (Gadgil, Kasturirangan).
Examine the role of local communities and governance in achieving sustainable development and conservation goals in the Western Ghats.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with misty mountains.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is one of the eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator program.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a key step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle.
• This technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by enabling multiple reuses of rocket stages, similar to commercial space ventures globally.
• The RLV program is central to ISRO's long-term strategy for sustained and cost-effective space exploration and commercial satellite launches.
• Scientists lauded the precision engineering and complex flight dynamics involved in achieving the desired landing parameters.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator test.
Focus on controlled landing experiment.
Goal: reduce cost of space access.
Significance for future space missions and commercial launches.
RLV-TD is a winged body aerospace vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its potential impact on global space access.
Analyze the technological challenges and strategic advantages associated with developing indigenous RLV capabilities.
Evaluate how RLV technology can contribute to India's economic growth through commercial space ventures and international collaborations.
Examine the future trajectory of ISRO's space exploration efforts in light of advancements in reusable launch systems.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) returning to Earth.
GS3.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested the RLV-TD (Hypersonic Flight Experiment) in 2016.

GS3.headline: Environmental Stalwart Saalumarada Thimmakka Dies at 114, Leaves Legacy of Greenery
GS3.explainer:
• Saalumarada Thimmakka, the revered environmentalist from Karnataka known for planting and nurturing thousands of trees, passed away at the remarkable age of 114.
• Affectionately known as 'Saalumarada' (rows of trees), she gained national and international recognition for her selfless efforts, particularly planting over 8,000 trees across 80 years, mainly banyan trees along a 4 km stretch of highway.
• Her work earned her the Padma Shri, India's fourth-highest civilian award, and numerous other accolades, inspiring countless individuals to take up environmental conservation.
• Thimmakka's life story is a testament to grassroots environmentalism and the profound impact a single individual can have on ecological restoration.
• Her legacy continues to motivate community-led tree plantation drives and environmental awareness campaigns.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Saalumarada Thimmakka: renowned environmentalist.
Known for planting thousands of trees, especially banyan.
Recipient of Padma Shri award.
Associated with Karnataka.
Symbol of grassroots environmentalism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of individual and community-led initiatives like Saalumarada Thimmakka's in addressing environmental degradation and promoting conservation.
Analyze how grassroots movements contribute to broader environmental policy and public awareness in India.
Evaluate the role of traditional knowledge and local communities in sustainable forest management and biodiversity protection, drawing insights from Thimmakka's life.
Examine the challenges faced by environmental activists and the importance of recognizing their contributions through awards like Padma Shri.
GS3.imageDescription: Saalumarada Thimmakka standing amidst lush green trees she planted.
GS3.trivia: Despite being illiterate, Thimmakka's dedication to planting trees earned her global recognition, including being named in BBC's list of 100 most influential women.

GS3.headline: India Hosts Global Summit on Digital Public Infrastructure, Advocates for Open-Source and Inclusive Models
GS3.explainer:
• India is hosting a high-level Global Summit on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), bringing together policymakers, tech experts, and international organizations to discuss future-proof digital ecosystems.
• The summit aims to share India's success story with its stack of DPI (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) and advocate for open-source, interoperable, and inclusive digital models globally.
• Discussions focused on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, accessible healthcare, education, and transparent governance in developing nations.
• Several countries expressed interest in adopting elements of the 'India Stack' to build their own digital public goods, facilitating cross-border digital payments and identity verification.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing ethical AI, data privacy, and global cooperation in building resilient digital infrastructures.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
IT, Computers, Robotics, Nanotechnology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights
